1. Brute Force Attack: This method involves trying all possible combinations of characters until the correct password is found. It is the simplest method but also the most time-consuming.

2. Dictionary Attack: This method involves using a dictionary of common words and trying them as passwords. It is faster than a brute force attack but still time-consuming.

3. Rainbow Table Attack: This method involves precomputing a table of hashes for common passwords and then comparing the table with the hash of the password being cracked. It is faster than a dictionary attack but still requires a lot of memory.

4. Phishing Attack: This method involves tricking the user into revealing their password. It is the fastest method but also the most difficult to implement.
